title: Separate Process into ProcessInput and ProcessResult — stop conflating input with computed output
description: scheduling.ts:16 Process type has input fields (id, name, arrivalTime, burstTime, priority) mixed with runtime state (remainingTime) and computed output (startTime?, completionTime?, waitTime?, turnaroundTime?, responseTime?). A developer creating a Process for fcfs() must set remainingTime (a runtime concept) in the input. Separate into ProcessInput (what user provides) and ProcessResult (what engine computes).
acceptanceCriteria: ProcessInput type: id, name, arrivalTime, burstTime, priority
ProcessResult extends ProcessInput with NON-optional: startTime, completionTime, waitTime, turnaroundTime, responseTime
Engine functions accept ProcessInput[], return ScheduleResult with processes: ProcessResult[]
OSModule.tsx creates ProcessInput[] (no remainingTime)
No optional computed fields in result — they're always filled after run

title: Create shared BaseOSEvent type for all step/event types
description: 6 different step types (ScheduleEvent, PageEvent, SyncEvent, MemoryAllocStep, AddressTranslation, BankersStep) all have tick and description but with no shared base. Step discriminant field varies: type vs action vs boolean flags. Create BaseOSEvent with tick+description, extend all step types from it. Create OSEvent discriminated union for the Event Log.
acceptanceCriteria: BaseOSEvent interface: { tick: number; description: string }
All 6 step types extend BaseOSEvent
OSEvent discriminated union: { concept: OSConcept; event: ScheduleEvent | PageEvent | ... }
Event Log component uses OSEvent union instead of 6-branch if/else
DeadlockResult.events uses named DeadlockEvent type instead of inline anonymous type

title: Add exhaustive switch pattern to all algorithm selection branches
description: runScheduling (OSModule.tsx:2117), runPageReplacement (2165), runAllocSimulation (2307), and runThreadSync (2060) use switch statements without exhaustive checks. Adding a new algorithm to SchedulingAlgo union won't cause a TypeScript error if you forget to add the case. Add `default: { const _exhaustive: never = algo; throw new Error(...) }` to all switches.
acceptanceCriteria: All algorithm switch statements have exhaustive default with never check
Adding a new value to SchedulingAlgo causes TypeScript error in runScheduling
Adding a new value to PageAlgo causes TypeScript error in runPageReplacement
Adding a new value to AllocAlgo causes TypeScript error in runAllocSimulation
Adding a new value to SyncPrimitive causes TypeScript error in runThreadSync

title: Type-narrow ScheduleResult.algorithm from string to SchedulingAlgo union
description: ScheduleResult.algorithm is typed as `string` (scheduling.ts:39) but it only ever holds values like 'FCFS', 'SJF', 'SRTF', 'Round Robin', 'Priority', 'MLFQ'. These are set by the finalise() function as hardcoded strings. Type should be a union of known algorithm names for type safety in consumers.
acceptanceCriteria: ScheduleResult.algorithm typed as SchedulingAlgorithmName union
type SchedulingAlgorithmName = 'FCFS' | 'SJF' | 'SRTF' | 'Round Robin' | ...
Same pattern for PageResult.algorithm and other result types
No string casts needed in consumers

title: Create ScheduleEventType enum for the 9-value event type union
description: ScheduleEvent.type is a 9-value string union: 'start' | 'resume' | 'preempt' | 'complete' | 'arrive' | 'idle' | 'demote' | 'boost' | 'io-return'. This appears in event creation (scheduling.ts, mlfq-scheduler.ts) and event rendering (OSModule.tsx:3788). An enum provides autocomplete, refactoring safety, and exhaustive checking.
acceptanceCriteria: enum ScheduleEventType { Start, Resume, Preempt, Complete, Arrive, Idle, Demote, Boost, IOReturn }
All event creation uses the enum, not string literals
Event log coloring uses switch(event.type) with exhaustive check
Same pattern for SyncEvent.action and BankersStep.action

title: Group related useState calls in useOSModule into typed state objects
description: useOSModule has 38 individual useState calls. Related state is scattered: allocSteps, allocStep, allocCompare, allocAlgo, allocTotalMem, allocRequests are 6 separate states for one concept. Group into typed state objects per concept: SchedulingState, PageReplacementState, DeadlockState, MemoryState, AllocState, SyncState.
acceptanceCriteria: Each concept's state grouped into a typed interface
SchedulingState: { algo, processes, result, comparisonResults }
AllocState: { algo, totalMem, requests, steps, step, compare }
Total useState calls reduced from 38 to ~10-12
Type safety: changing algo auto-suggests valid state fields
No functional regression

title: Extract named DeadlockEvent type from anonymous inline type in DeadlockResult
description: DeadlockResult.events at deadlock.ts:39-50 uses an anonymous inline type: Array<{ tick, type, processId, resourceId, description }>. This should be a named DeadlockEvent interface for reusability, readability, and consistency with other event types (ScheduleEvent, PageEvent, etc.).
acceptanceCriteria: New interface DeadlockEvent { tick, type: DeadlockEventType, processId, resourceId, description }
DeadlockResult.events typed as DeadlockEvent[]
DeadlockEvent exported from deadlock.ts and re-exported from index.ts
OSModule.tsx imports DeadlockEvent type
